claim_boundary: This map enumerates foreseeable failure classes for the next tranche. It is preventative, not promotional.

rows.false_positive_trigger: R5 still says router_superiority_earned=false but downstream narration says otherwise.
rows.void_condition: Any result interpreted as superiority without a fresh lawful R5 earn.
rows.what_can_fail: Specialist-family signal is mistaken for branch-level superiority.

rows.false_positive_trigger: Pressure sweeps modify counted claims without passing transfer guard.
rows.void_condition: Any counted-lane result derived directly from crucible output without named transfer surfaces.
rows.what_can_fail: Lab crucible output is allowed to impersonate counted proof.

rows.false_positive_trigger: Route value rises but rightful control families stop holding.
rows.what_can_fail: Static and abstention controls drift while specialists improve.

rows.false_positive_trigger: Composite overload results appear before single-axis evidence exists.
rows.void_condition: No named single-axis wedge sharpening can be attached to the result.
rows.what_can_fail: Too many pressure axes move at once and wedge sharpening becomes uninterpretable.

rows.false_positive_trigger: Gate E/F, commercialization, or learned-router claims appear while R6 remains blocked.
rows.void_condition: Any output exceeds current verdict grammar and counted-lane ceiling.
rows.what_can_fail: The branch starts speaking beyond its actual proof class.
